reasoning: The Polymarket price on April 17, 2025 was 10%. Information from late March and April 2025 shows that the Federal Reserve held rates steady (4.25-4.50%) in both January and March. The median Fed 'dot plot' from March projects 50 basis points of total easing in 2025 (amounting to two 25-bps cuts). Meanwhile, fixed income markets (per CME FedWatch Tool data in April) broadly price in between 2 to 4 rate cuts for the entirety of 2025. Exactly 1 rate cut implies a very precise scenario where the Fed begins easing but immediately stops after a single 25 bps move, or delays easing until the very end of the year and makes only one move. Given that both '0 cuts' and '2+ cuts' encompass broader ranges of macro outcomes, the probability mass for exactly 1 cut remains sandwiched as a relatively uncommon scenario. There have been no drastic economic shifts in the past 10 days to significantly alter the baseline established by the Polymarket 10% price, keeping the probability around 10-12%.
